failedAssertions.reason: The dashboard does render the nine standards, but it does not expose the required compliant/partially-compliant/non-compliant states or an expanded evidence view with timestamps and authors.
Evidence can only be entered from the standard page via a generic Record ID modal; the record detail page lacks the required linking action and the bidirectional linkage badge.
The children's register route is present but only renders an empty state with no user-facing admission, departure, or filter workflow.
The legal-status workflow is not testable through the real UI because the LAC overview is empty and the creation route returns 404.
No social worker or IRO contact surface is rendered on the child profile, and the missing LAC creation route prevents populating or changing those details.
The placement-plan list exists only as an empty state, the creation route is missing, and the dashboard does not expose the required 5-working-day countdown or review alerts.
The decline path does not archive declined referrals; Lily Shaw remained on the main admissions list instead of moving to an archive view.
The concern form is not submittable from the real UI, and the 'body map' surface is only a record-ID field rather than an annotation tool.
Restricted-access enforcement is missing on the LADO route for standard staff, and neither the LADO nor Section 47 workflows are implemented beyond placeholder pages.
No discoverable Philomena profile surface exists in the real UI, so the required profile fields, printable/exportable police handover page, and photo-age alert could not be reached or verified.
The real UI does not expose a Missing Episode creation or escalation surface, so staff cannot record absence, trigger timestamped escalation steps, or record the child's return.

No Return Home Interview task or route is exposed in the real UI, so the required auto-created RHI, 72-hour countdown, escalation to the Responsible Individual, and chronology linkage cannot be performed.

The contact-management UI is only a placeholder shell: approved contacts cannot be added, court-order restrictions cannot be entered, and the required scheduling enforcement/manager override workflow is absent.
The page exposes labels for scheduling, recording, and compliance only; the actual contact-visit workflow is not implemented in the user-facing UI.
The session form is only partially structured, actions cannot be assigned to specific staff, and the dashboard exposes no session-compliance or overdue-action tracking.
Debriefs are not mandatory, no debrief tasks are created, and the tested UI lacks management sign-off and restraint-register views.
Prohibited sanctions are warn-only and still recordable, which does not satisfy the required safeguard.
The tested owner session could not open the new-visitor page and the visitor-log surface does not expose the required search/filter controls.
Basic wishes capture exists, but there is no discovered care-decision linkage prompt and no visible auto-generated LAC review summary.
No user-facing children's meetings surface was present in the tested application.
Complaint handling is not implemented as a dedicated user-facing workflow; the tested UI lacks advocacy prompts, staged statuses, satisfaction capture, and report output.
The care-notes surface is a partial running record only; it lacks several required children-home fields and provides manual handover text instead of an auto-generated handover summary.

blockedAssertions.blockedBy: Blocked because the DSL review user surface is not implemented beyond a placeholder, and no submitted concern exists for review because VAL-CHILD-008 failed.
Blocked because the chronology page is not implemented beyond a placeholder and no concern record was created to feed it.
Blocked by the non-functional concern submission surface and missing DSL review/chronology implementations.

appliedUpdates.description: Documented that children's-home onboarding may normalize requested org slugs, so validators should follow the actual redirected slug after onboarding.
